comment.body: Doc you'll have to download this , most recent computers with their operating systems should support this file type.  Inside will contain a text file.  You will need to open this text file copy all of the text lines. Go to your web browser and open trading view.  Go to one of your charts and at the bottom is a pinescript editor.  Click that button, select create a new script and add paste the text in here.  Make sure the default text in line editor is overwritten .  Click add to chart. And it should populate
comment.raw_body: Doc you'll have to download this , most recent computers with their operating systems should support this file type.  Inside will contain a text file. comment.body:
Alma, more of a philosophical question, but do you believe markets are a random walk or are they order within chaos? 

On one hand vol models assume a normal distribution and a degree of randomness with Brownian motion but at the same time there are traders than can consistently harvest profits using some sort of edge but eventually those edges will degrade as time goes on. Which is it to you?

comment.body:
While we are waiting for Alma to open Q&A does anyone have insight to this question?  

In the Friday post where Alma said the two important flip points for gamma were 5890 and 5854. What was the important characteristic of both of those areas? I see at 5890 it was near zero speed on the polynomial trendline and also very steeply positive speed locally.   

Was 5854 a zero speed area also plotted on a higher order polynomial? Is it that both those strikes had zero gamma exposure in the midst of steep speed curves?

Basically. How did Alma reason that 5854 was the pivot for squeeze?
